resolution: Use a side-effect import in Astro browser scripts: import "hyperscript.org". Keep src/types/hyperscript.d.ts generic instead of declaring browserInit().

title: Pilot Tina visual editing on Spanish home
rationale: A narrow /es/ home-page pilot verifies the Astro-specific Tina island flow on Cloudflare before extending the same pattern to the rest of the site.

title: Read Tina credentials from Astro runtime env on Cloudflare Pages
rationale: The project removed wrangler.jsonc so Cloudflare Pages variables stay dashboard-managed. Tina SSR data loading therefore passes Astro.locals.runtime.env into the Tina client, with import.meta.env only as local development fallback, so dashboard secrets like TINA_CLIENT_ID and TINA_TOKEN are available at runtime without repo-managed env config.

title: Canonical editorial routes use studio, works, and methods
rationale: Public URLs now match the visible navigation vocabulary across all locales. Legacy about, projects, and services routes remain as permanent redirects; internal content filenames stay unchanged to preserve Tina collection stability.

name: Statically extractable Alpine and Tina presentation tokens
description: Write complete UnoCSS utility literals in Astro and Alpine bindings, use static maps for finite computed variants, and derive TinaCMS choices plus the UnoCSS safelist from one bounded token list. Add filesystem extraction only for source files that contain literal response-fragment classes; runtime-generated class names are prohibited.

title: Retire hyperscript from the Senshac frontend
rationale: Hyperscript has no behavioral usage in the repository and is loaded globally. Alpine covers component-local state and HTMX covers server interaction, so removing hyperscript reduces client JavaScript and eliminates an overlapping behavior language.

name: alpine-architecture
description: Alpine.js Component Initialization: Reusable Alpine components (e.g., carousel, cursor) are registered in src/utils/alpine.ts via Alpine.data() and Alpine.directive(). They are initialized globally in Base.astro to ensure they are available to all Astro components. Avoid inline vanilla JS event listeners for layout behavior; prefer scoped Alpine directives (x-data, x-reveal) to prevent hydration/SSR mismatches and duplicate logic.
